name: Conopophagidae
description: Gnateaters are small, plump, short-tailed birds of the Neotropical forest understory. They are named for their insectivorous diet and spend most of their time foraging close to the ground.

children.name: Conopophaga
children.description: Conopophaga is the primary genus of gnateaters, found in lowland and montane forests across South America.

children.name: Conopophaga lineata
children.description: The rufous gnateater is a small, warm-brown bird of the Atlantic Forest of eastern Brazil. Males have a distinctive white postocular stripe and a rufous breast.

children.name: Conopophaga aurita
children.description: The chestnut-belted gnateater has a rich chestnut breast band and is found in lowland Amazonian forests. It is a shy, skulking bird that is more often heard than seen.

children.name: Conopophaga melanogaster
children.description: The black-bellied gnateater is a distinctive species with a black belly and chestnut crown, endemic to the southern Amazon basin. It inhabits terra firme forests near streams.

children.name: Conopophaga roberti
children.description: The hooded gnateater is named for its black hood contrasting with a white throat, found in the lower Amazon region. It is a rare and locally distributed species of humid lowland forests.
